Title: Healthy Home project
Description: Healthy Home project: This project offers people with a long-term health condition help with cold homes. The project helps improve home insulation and heating in order to keep your home warm and you healthy. You could be eligible for a free new energy efficient boiler, loft or cavity wall insulation or other energy saving measures subject to a survey by one of the Healthy Home team. You’ll receive advice to help you improve the efficiency of your heating system to reduce your fuel bills. The project also aims to keep you healthy and independent in your own home, and to do this we may offer you services run by other trusted organisations in your area. To find out if you’re eligible for the project email: healthyhome@derbyshire.gov.uk or tel: 01629 536919.

Title: Winter pressures
Description: Winter pressures - pathway for professionals: We recognise the challenges affecting everyone during the winter, but these challenges have been intensified this year by the impact of the current financial situation. People are affected in so many ways, this could be financially or it could have impacted on their health and wellbeing as they may have become lonely and isolated. The colder months can impact on everyone especially the older and vulnerable members of our community or those living with ill health or long-term conditions.

Description: The Home Office 2030 Digital Strategy outlines how the department will use AI, data, and modern digital systems to improve public services, strengthen security, enhance efficiency, and build a more agile, cost-effective, and digitally skilled organisation.

Title: Adult Social Care and Public Health Prevention Strategy
Description: Derbyshire’s Adult Social Care and Public Health Joint Prevention Strategy 2025–2027 sets out a partnership approach to improving wellbeing and independence by shifting towards earlier intervention, tackling loneliness and unmet need, and building on the strengths of communities, local services, and the voluntary sector.

Title: Get Britain Working White Paper
Description: Sets out the UK Government’s proposals to reform employment, health and skills support to tackle economic inactivity and help more people into good work.

Title: Get East Midlands Working Plan
Description: A 10-year regional plan intended to help more people across the East Midlands access good jobs, develop skills, and improve health and wellbeing.

Title: Research & Evidence IPS Grow
Description: Summarises the evidence base for Individual Placement and Support, highlighting that the “place then train” approach is more effective than traditional vocational approaches for helping people into work.

Title: IPS in the UK: Improving employment support in mental health services
Description: Explains the IPS model, outlines the supporting evidence, and describes how Centre for Mental Health has supported IPS implementation in UK mental health services.

Title: Active labor market as an instrument to reduce unemployment
Description: Examines the long-run effects of active labour market policy across nine OECD countries and argues that such policies can reduce unemployment.

Title: Disability Employment Service
Description: Derbyshire County Council service page outlining free employment support for Derbyshire residents aged 18+ with a disability or long-term health condition.

Title: Disability Employer Engagement Derbyshire (DEED)
Description: Derbyshire County Council page describing support for employers who want to recruit or retain people with disabilities or long-term health conditions.

Title: Employing disabled people and people with health conditions
Description: GOV.UK guidance for employers on recruiting, supporting and retaining disabled people and people with long-term health conditions at work.

Title: Preventing disability discrimination
Description: Acas advice page setting out practical steps employers can take to prevent disability discrimination and make workplaces more inclusive.

Title: Access to Work factsheet for employers
Description: GOV.UK factsheet explaining how Access to Work can help employers and employees with practical and financial support in the workplace.

Title: Youth employment support
Description: Derbyshire County Council page about YES Derbyshire, a service helping 16 to 24 year olds access employment, training and related support.
